channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India Adopts Cognitive Electronic Warfare to Defeat SAR Satellites with “Ghosting” and “Erasure” Deception Techniques
item.description: In the aftermath of Operation Sindoor in May 2025, India’s electronic warfare doctrine has undergone a marked transition from conventional noise-based jamming to a far more sophisticated paradigm centered on cognitive electronic warfare. At the core of this shift is the deployment of SAR Active Decoy systems—also referred to as Coherent Deception Jamming—designed not to merely obscure signals, but to actively manipulate what enemy sensors perceive, particularly synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellites.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BluJ Aerospace’s REACH Drone Promises Sub-1 Hour Offshore Delivery, Disrupting Oil & Gas Logistics Bottlenecks
item.description: India’s deepwater oil and gas sector may be on the cusp of a logistics transformation as BluJ Aerospace Private Limited unveils its REACH autonomous delivery platform, designed to cut critical offshore supply times from 10–15 hours to under one hour. In an industry where downtime can translate into losses running into millions of dollars per hour, the ability to rapidly transport essential components such as valves, sensors, and mission-critical hardware directly from mainland facilities to offshore rigs represents a structural shift in operational efficiency.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tejas Mk2’s “Stealth-First” Design: How India’s MWF Could Redefine Low Observability in the 4.5-Gen Fighter Class
item.description: The LCA Tejas Mk2, positioned as India’s Medium Weight Fighter (MWF), is being engineered with a clear emphasis on low observability, signaling a doctrinal shift in how 4.5-generation combat aircraft are designed in the country. While it does not qualify as a fifth-generation stealth platform like the AMCA, the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) is embedding a range of radar cross-section (RCS) reduction techniques that could make the Mk2 one of the least detectable fighters in its category.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NavIC Crisis Rekindles Debate: Should ISRO Exit Defence Operations as Private Space Firms Rise?
item.description: The operational disruption of India’s NavIC navigation constellation in early 2026 has triggered a serious policy debate on whether ISRO should continue handling defence-oriented space assets or transition into a pure research and deep-space agency. The issue is no longer theoretical. With the constellation reportedly slipping below the minimum satellite threshold required for reliable 3D positioning, India is once again confronting the very vulnerability NavIC was designed to eliminate—dependence on foreign navigation systems during conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: “Future Wars Will Be Won in Space”: DRDO Chief Flags Urgency as India’s Private Space Boom Accelerates Military Transformation
item.description: DRDO Chairman Dr. Samir V Kamat’s assertion that space will be a domain in future warfare is less a warning and more a recognition of an ongoing structural shift in India’s strategic ecosystem. The traditional separation between civilian space programs and military applications is rapidly eroding, and India is now moving toward a whole-of-nation architecture where government agencies, private industry, and the armed forces are converging to build space-based capabilities at scale.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Zen Technologies Secures Arms Licence to Manufacture Air Defence Cannons for Counter-Drone Warfare
item.description: Zen Technologies Limited has received a government-issued arms manufacturing licence, enabling the Hyderabad-based firm to enter the production of air defence cannons tailored for counter-drone and low-altitude threat environments. The move marks a significant expansion for the company, which has traditionally focused on combat training and simulation systems, into frontline defence hardware manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Reliance Defence Becomes First Private Indian Firm to Secure ARDE Authentication for 155mm Ammunition
item.description: Reliance Infrastructure, through its subsidiary Reliance Defence Limited, has achieved a notable milestone in India’s defence manufacturing ecosystem by becoming the first private sector company to receive authentication from the Armament Research and Development Establishment (ARDE) for indigenous 155mm artillery ammunition. The development marks a significant step forward for private industry participation in a domain traditionally dominated by state-run entities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: We Are Apolitical And Will Remain So: General Naravane Slams Attempts To Link Army With Politics
item.description: Former Indian Army Chief (retired) General Manoj Mukund Naravane has emphasised the significant distinction between politics and the Indian armed forces, criticising efforts to link the military to political matters.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Merchant Navy Officer From Rajasthan On Vessel Seized By Iran: Family
item.description: An India-bound container vessel was seized by Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) in the Strait of Hormuz, with a merchant navy officer from Rajasthan’s Sri Ganganagar district among the crew, his family said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Highly Vulnerable’ Pak Not On Middle East Battlefield But Exposed To Every Ripple It Creates: Report
item.description: Pakistan may not be a direct participant in the ongoing tensions in the Middle East, but its weak economic structure makes it highly vulnerable to global shocks arising from this conflict, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, US Discuss Deepening Strategic Ties, Reaffirm Commitment Towards Indo-Pacific Security
item.description: Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) General Anil Chauhan held discussions with a senior US Indo-Pacific Command official, reinforcing the expanding strategic alignment between the two nations in terms of regional security.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Haven’t Seen Myself, Can’t Say Where It Came From: Ex-Army Chief On Rahul’s Claims About Unpublished Book
item.description: Former Indian Army Chief, General (retired) Manoj Mukund Naravane said that he has not seen his memoir ‘Four Stars of Destiny’ in a published form and has no answers to how the Lok Sabha Leader of Opposition (LoP) Rahul Gandhi got access to it.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Babbar Khalsa’s New Strategy: Radicalise Rural Punjab, Hit Urban Targets
item.description: Intelligence agencies have issued a fresh alert regarding plans by the banned outfit Babbar Khalsa International to revive the Khalistan movement in Punjab, with a new strategy focused on rural radicalisation and low-intensity attacks in urban centres, officials familiar with the development have said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Security Forces Arrest Three ULFA-I Members In Nagaland
item.description: Security forces have apprehended three cadres of the banned United Liberation Front of Asom (Independent) [ULFA-I] during a counter-insurgency operation in Nagaland’s Mon district, officials said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Manipur: Security Forces Intensify Operations In Ukhrul After Deadly Clash, Appeal For Peace
item.description: A day after three people were killed and several others injured in a violent clash in Manipur’s Ukhrul district, central and state security forces on Saturday intensified operations to apprehend those involved and prevent further escalation, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin
